description_long: This is a pair of heavy-duty bolt cutters with robust, elongated handles made of wood, showing signs of wear and a natural brown patina. The handles are straight and cylindrical, attached to a solid, dark metal head with a compound pivot mechanism. The jaws are thick, short, and flat-edged, designed for cutting through metal rods, bolts, or chains, and are joined by large, visible bolts. The metal head features a matte, slightly rough finish, with some visible fasteners and mechanical joints. The overall construction suggests durability and strength, suitable for demanding urban tasks such as cutting locks or fencing. No visible branding or logos are present on the tool.
description: A rugged pair of bolt cutters with worn wooden handles and a sturdy, dark metal cutting head, designed for heavy-duty cutting tasks.
description_view_0: The tool is seen from the side, showing both wooden handles splayed apart and the full profile of the metal cutting jaws and pivot.
description_view_1: A top-down view reveals the cylindrical shape of one handle and the narrow profile of the metal head.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays the other face of the tool, mirroring the first side with visible bolts and the full jaw profile.
description_view_3: A bottom-up view focuses on the end of one handle and the compact, aligned form of the jaws.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handles, uninsulated, with a natural finish.
attribute_jaw_design: Short, flat-edged cutting jaws with a robust, blunt geometry for cutting metal; no serrations or notches visible.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Compound pivot mechanism with large bolts, providing increased cutting force; fixed jaw width.
attribute_grip_pattern: Handles appear smooth with visible wood grain, lacking additional grip pattern or rubberized texture.
